title: Create initial repository skeleton and module layout
problem: The project needs a KMP repository layout that can support a small stable core plus optional provider, storage, testkit, and Meeseeks modules.
proposal: Create Gradle/KMP skeleton with core, testkit, provider-openai-compatible placeholder, samples, docs, and CI-ready build structure.
acceptance: Placeholder provider module exists but does not affect core dependencies.

title: Define core request and key model
problem: Inference requests need a stable shape that captures key, input, output type, privacy, cache, policy, timeout, prompt version, and metadata.
proposal: Implement `InferenceRequest`, `InferenceKey`, `InferenceInput`, `OutputSpec`, `PromptSpec`, and basic convenience builders.
acceptance: Text and message inputs are supported.
JSON/typed output spec API is sketched or implemented behind serialization.
Request has privacy and cache defaults.
Unit tests cover request creation and equality/fingerprint-relevant fields.

title: Define provider contract and provider metadata
problem: Provider adapters need a common contract for availability, capabilities, streaming execution, and metadata.
proposal: Implement `InferenceProvider`, `ProviderId`, `ProviderKind`, `ProviderAvailability`, `Capability`, `CapabilityReport`, `ProviderMetadata`, `ProviderEvent`, and `ProviderError`.
acceptance: Providers can report availability without executing inference.
Providers can report capability support for a request.
Provider errors map to stable categories.

title: Implement streaming-first InferenceStore API
problem: Feature code needs a single API for streaming and non-streaming inference.
proposal: Implement `InferenceStore.stream(request)` and `InferenceStore.generate(request)` in core, backed by a simple execution controller.
acceptance: `generate` collects stream and returns final result.
Cancellation of collection cancels provider execution where possible.
Event sequence includes Started, ProviderSelected, Token, Done/Failed.
Unit tests cover success, failure, and cancellation.

title: Implement route trace and event model
problem: Users need to know which provider served an inference request and why fallback occurred.
proposal: Add `InferenceEvent`, `InferenceResult`, `RouteTrace`, `ProviderAttemptTrace`, and route metadata to all results.
acceptance: Every provider attempt is captured with provider ID/kind/model metadata when available.

title: Implement built-in local/cloud policy presets
problem: The MVP needs simple but useful policies that prove the local/cloud routing model.
proposal: Implement `localOnly`, `cloudOnly`, `preferLocalThenCloud`, `preferCloudThenLocal`, and `validateLocalRepairWithCloud`.
acceptance: Policies choose providers based on kind, availability, and capabilities.
Privacy guardrails cannot be bypassed by policy.
Docs show when to use each policy.

title: Add privacy policy model and enforcement
problem: Cloud fallback must not happen accidentally for sensitive/local-only requests.
proposal: Implement `PrivacyPolicy`, privacy classifications, cloud permission, persistence permission, telemetry permission, and enforcement in execution controller.
acceptance: Execution controller enforces privacy independent of policy.
Tests prove denied providers were not invoked.
Monitor events are redacted by default.

title: Implement output validators and validation-triggered fallback
problem: Local model output may be malformed or inadequate; validation should be able to trigger fallback or repair.
proposal: Implement `OutputValidator`, `ValidationResult`, predicate validator helpers, and execution logic for fallback on validation failure.
acceptance: Predicate validators can pass/fail final output.
Validation result is included in route trace.
Policy can fall back on validation failure.
Schema/JSON validation has either initial implementation or RFC stub.
Tests cover local invalid -> cloud repair.

title: Create testkit fake provider and route assertions
problem: Developers need deterministic tests without real models or cloud calls.
proposal: Implement `FakeInferenceProvider`, scripted responses, failure injection, streaming scripts, and route assertion helpers.
acceptance: Fake provider can fail with stable error categories.

title: Add request fingerprinting
problem: Caching and dedupe require a stable fingerprint that accounts for input, prompt, output, privacy, and policy-relevant fields.
proposal: Implement `InferenceFingerprint` and default fingerprinting strategy with extension hooks.
acceptance: Fingerprint includes key, input hash, prompt version, output version, privacy class, and policy version if present.
Raw input is not stored in the fingerprint.

title: Implement request deduplication
problem: Equivalent concurrent requests should not cause duplicate provider invocations when policy allows sharing.
proposal: Add in-flight request coalescing keyed by fingerprint and compatible policy/cache settings.
acceptance: Concurrent equivalent requests share one provider call.
Requests with dedupe disabled do not share.
Cancellation of one collector does not cancel shared work while another collector remains.
Tests cover success, failure, and cancellation.

title: Define cache and artifact store interfaces
problem: The core needs cache/source-of-truth-like hooks without overcommitting to a persistent implementation.
proposal: Add `InferenceCache`, `InferenceArtifactStore`, `InferenceArtifact`, `CachePolicy`, and `CacheOutcome` interfaces/types.
acceptance: Core can read before provider execution when cache policy allows.
Core can write successful results when cache policy allows.
Privacy policy can disable prompt/output persistence.
Docs explain why this is not exactly Store SourceOfTruth.

title: Implement in-memory cache
problem: A small in-memory cache is needed for demos, tests, and repeated UI calls.
proposal: Implement `MemoryInferenceCache` with TTL and clear APIs.
acceptance: Cache hit returns artifact without provider call.

title: Implement monitor hooks
problem: Apps need route, latency, fallback, and validation telemetry.
proposal: Add `InferenceMonitor`, `MonitorEvent`, redaction defaults, and execution controller callbacks.
acceptance: Monitor receives route selected/provider attempt/fallback/validation events.
Monitor events contain no raw prompt/output by default.
Tests cover event order and redaction.

title: Build OpenAI-compatible provider adapter
problem: The MVP needs a real cloud/remote provider adapter that works with many OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
proposal: Implement `inferencestore-provider-openai-compatible` using configurable base URL, API key provider, model name, and streaming support.
acceptance: Adapter supports streaming where endpoint supports it.
Adapter maps rate limit, timeout, network, and server errors.
Adapter redacts secrets and prompts by default.
Mock-server tests cover success, streaming, and error mapping.

title: Create sample: private note summarization
problem: The project needs a concrete demo that explains the value better than architecture docs.
proposal: Build a sample note summarizer using fake local provider, OpenAI-compatible cloud provider, privacy toggle, and route trace display.
acceptance: Sample runs on JVM or Android initially.
Local unavailable -> cloud fallback scenario works.
Route trace is visible in logs or UI.

title: Write Quickstart documentation
problem: Developers need a runnable path from install to first routed inference request.
proposal: Create Quickstart with dependency setup, fake provider, OpenAI-compatible provider, policy, streaming, and tests.
acceptance: Quickstart includes Gradle version catalog and Kotlin DSL examples.
All code snippets compile in sample/test.

title: Write provider adapter authoring guide
problem: Runtime maintainers need clear guidance for writing adapters.
proposal: Document provider contract, availability/capability mapping, streaming, cancellation, error mapping, privacy, and tests.
acceptance: Guide explains adapter responsibilities and non-responsibilities.

title: Design JSON/schema structured output support
problem: Structured output is a major use case, but provider support varies and schema validation can be complex.
proposal: Write RFC and initial API for JSON/schema validation, parsing, and repair fallback.
acceptance: RFC covers schema-constrained providers vs post-hoc validation.
RFC covers streamed partial JSON as post-MVP.
Initial API can validate final JSON text.
Tests demonstrate malformed local JSON -> cloud repair.

title: Add route journal interface
problem: Policies may need recent failure/cooldown information to avoid repeatedly choosing bad providers.
proposal: Define `RouteJournal` and in-memory implementation for provider attempt history, failures, and cooldowns.
acceptance: Recent failures can be queried by provider.

title: Add timeout and retry semantics
problem: Provider calls need predictable timeout and retry behavior before fallback.
proposal: Add attempt timeout support and stable retry/fallback categories. Keep retry minimal in core; prefer fallback over hidden retries.
acceptance: Timeout maps to stable error/fallback reason.
Retries are explicit and observable if implemented.

title: Evaluate first local adapter choice
problem: The first real local adapter should maximize learning without trapping the project in runtime churn.
proposal: Compare Apple Foundation Models, Firebase AI Logic on-device/Android, Llamatik, Cactus, LiteRT-LM, and MLC LLM for first adapter.
acceptance: Comparison matrix includes platform support, licensing, maturity, API stability, streaming, structured output, model management, and KMP fit.
Adapter issue is created for the selected choice.
Risks and deferred candidates are documented.

title: Prototype Android local/platform adapter
problem: The project needs one real Android local or platform inference path to prove value on-device.
proposal: Implement experimental Android adapter based on selected runtime/platform path.
acceptance: Availability and capability mapping are implemented.
Basic text generation works on supported environment or sample stub documents setup.
Unsupported devices/models return stable availability/capability reasons.

title: Prototype iOS local/platform adapter
problem: The project needs an iOS path to prove KMP value beyond Android/JVM.
proposal: Implement experimental iOS adapter based on selected platform/runtime path, likely behind expect/actual and Swift interop as needed.
acceptance: Setup docs explain required OS/device constraints.

title: Create cross-platform sample app
problem: A KMP library needs a mobile sample that demonstrates shared request/policy/test code and platform-specific providers.
proposal: Build a minimal Android/iOS sample using common inference code and platform-specific provider modules.
acceptance: Common code defines request and policy.
Android provider configured in Android app.
iOS provider configured in iOS app.
Route trace shows actual provider used.

title: Add SQLDelight artifact store prototype
problem: Early adopters may need persistent artifacts and route traces.
proposal: Implement SQLDelight-backed artifact store and route journal prototype.
acceptance: Artifact records persist across process restart.

title: Add OpenTelemetry monitor exporter
problem: Production teams need to export inference route metrics to their observability stack.
proposal: Implement optional OpenTelemetry exporter module for route spans and metrics.
acceptance: Request route is represented as span(s).
Docs include setup and attribute list.

title: Create Meeseeks provider inventory refresh worker
problem: Foreground route planning benefits from cached provider/model availability, but probing every time can be expensive.
proposal: Add optional Meeseeks worker that refreshes provider inventory in the background.
acceptance: Tests use fake providers and fake Meeseeks/runtime where possible.

title: Create Meeseeks model warmup worker
problem: Local models may have high first-token latency unless warmed before use.
proposal: Define optional provider lifecycle interface and Meeseeks worker for model warmup.
acceptance: Provider lifecycle interface includes warmup if supported.
Warmup failures are recorded without crashing.

title: Create Meeseeks telemetry upload worker
problem: Apps may want to batch and retry telemetry upload without impacting foreground inference.
proposal: Add optional worker that uploads redacted route telemetry batches.
acceptance: Telemetry payload excludes raw prompt/output by default.

title: Write security and privacy guide
problem: Users need clear guidance on privacy policies, cloud fallback, logging, persistence, and telemetry.
proposal: Write a security/privacy guide with defaults, examples, test assertions, and adapter checklist.

title: Create competitive landscape document
problem: The project needs a clear explanation of why it exists alongside Firebase AI Logic, Apple Foundation Models, LiteRT-LM, ExecuTorch, MLC, Llamatik, and Cactus.
proposal: Write comparison matrix and positioning doc focused on orchestration vs runtime execution.
acceptance: Matrix includes runtime/provider projects and their strengths.

title: Open public RFC discussion for core API
problem: The API should be pressure-tested before implementation hardens.
proposal: Publish RFC-0001 and RFC-0002 as GitHub discussions and solicit feedback from Store, KMP, and local inference communities.

title: Build validation demo with fake providers
problem: Potential users need to see the value without waiting for real provider adapters.
proposal: Create runnable JVM/CLI demo using fake local/cloud providers and three route scenarios.
acceptance: Demo shows local unavailable -> cloud fallback.
Demo shows local schema invalid -> cloud repair.

title: Set up release automation and snapshot publishing
problem: Early adopters need reproducible artifacts and snapshots.
proposal: Configure CI, signing/publishing path, changelog, versioning, and release notes.
acceptance: Version catalog examples use published coordinates.

title: Create documentation site skeleton
problem: The project needs docs similar in quality and navigability to Store/Meeseeks.
proposal: Set up docs site skeleton with sections for get started, concepts, guides, adapters, testing, and security.
acceptance: Concept pages exist for store, provider, policy, validator, artifact store, monitor.
